_comment: Benchmark manifest. Two cohorts label code by QUALITY, not authorship:
- 'clean'  = high-quality, widely-respected Python a good engineer is proud of.
- 'slop'   = low-quality / vibe-coded Python that is dense with smells.
Vibe-coded repos are just a convenient SOURCE of slop; any rule we mine from this
data must still flag BADNESS, never AI authorship (see slop-is-badness-not-provenance).
Each entry: name, url, ref (tag/branch/sha), cohort, paths (dirs to measure).
sloplint only reads .py files, so paths ['.'] safely measures the Python subset of a
mixed-language repo. run_manifest.json records the resolved sha after each run, so a run
reproduces even from a moving branch. Edit freely.
HOW THE slop[] REPOS WERE PICKED (see find_slop_gharchive.py):
1. DISCOVER candidates from GH Archive (https://data.gharchive.org) hourly dumps:
stream a sample of hours, grep PushEvent payloads for the AI-authorship trailers
'Generated with Claude Code' / 'Co-Authored-By: Claude' / 'Made with Cursor'.
GH Archive needs no auth and is NOT subject to GitHub's commit-search rate limit.
2. SCORE each candidate with the GitHub CORE API only (5000/hr, never search):
- /repos/{r}/languages          -> python_frac (keep >= 0.6)
- /repos/{r}                     -> size_kb (floor + cap, skip monster trees)
- paginate /repos/{r}/commits   -> ai_frac = WHOLE-HISTORY share of commits
carrying a trailer (we trust whole-history, not just the last 100 commits).
Keep ai_frac >= ~0.4 over a mature history => genuinely vibe-coded.
Provenance only SELECTS candidates; each then earns 'slop' by measuring badly.
Established/popular repos that merely use AI assist are NOT slop and are excluded
(e.g. borgbackup/borg 13k stars, getsentry/skills) per slop-is-badness-not-provenance.
Each slop entry records its measured python_frac, ai_frac, total_commits, size_kb.
